div1.entryFree:
Fold, subst. 1) the doubling of cloth, the complication, plait: to dismantle so many --s of favour, Lr. I, 1, 221 (a favour which covered Cordelia like a wide garment?).
2) the roll of a snake: an adder wreathed up in fatal --s, Ven. 879.
3) the embrace: the weak wanton Cupid shall from your neck unloose his amorous f. Troil. III, 3, 223.
